title: Starting at school - Ministry of Education
content:
Te tīmatatanga ki te kura
Find out about what your child will need at school and how to enrol them.
- Learning and curriculum 1
- Legal and compliance 1
- Te uru ā-rōpū mō ngā tamariki e tīmata ana ki te kura Cohort or group entry for children starting school Cohort entry is where children start school for the first time in groups on set dates, rather than on or after their fifth birthday.
Cohort entry is where children start school for the first time in groups on set dates, rather than on or after their fifth birthday.
- Ngā utu me ngā koha ā-kura School costs and donations Students | ākonga have a right to free education in New Zealand, but there can be some costs. Schools and kura may also ask for donations.
Students | ākonga have a right to free education in New Zealand, but there can be some costs. Schools and kura may also ask for donations.
- Te whakauru i ngā tamariki kei Aotearoa e noho taihara ana School enrolment for children who are unlawfully in New Zealand If your child does not have a valid visa to be in New Zealand, they might still be able to go to school if they get approval from us.
If your child does not have a valid visa to be in New Zealand, they might still be able to go to school if they get approval from us.
- Ngā mōtika whakauru ki te kura mō ngā ākonga School enrolment rights for students All children, including ākonga | students with disabilities, have the right to enrol at school, attend full-time and participate in school activities.
All children, including ākonga | students with disabilities, have the right to enrol at school, attend full-time and participate in school activities.
- Ngā kaupapa rohenga ā-kura, me ngā kaupapa whakauru School zones and enrolment schemes Some schools and kura have enrolment schemes. This means only students who live in a specific area around the school are guaranteed a place.
Some schools and kura have enrolment schemes. This means only students who live in a specific area around the school are guaranteed a place.
- Ngā momo mātauranga paetahi, me ngā mātauranga paerua Types of primary and secondary education Find out about different types of schools in New Zealand and how they work.
Find out about different types of schools in New Zealand and how they work.
